behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is local lint logic over template text (no harness wiring required). Candidate tests: Unit: feed template string containing copied phase instruction, assert linter error; catches missed leakage rule. Integration alternative: run full harness lint stage, assert failure; catches wiring issues but is not required for this behavior. Rejection gate: behavior says "static prompt-template lint check," which disqualifies needing System/Agentic/Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Setup: pass a template fixture containing a verbatim phase-instruction snippet to the lint function. Assertion: lint returns a specific leakage violation. Failure caught: rule regression that allows copied SKILL.md phase text.

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation and fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at module handoff (template + interpolator + fixture loader + assembler), so Unit is too low. Candidate tests: Integration: run real prompt assembly and assert leakage detector finds none; catches interpolation leakage. System alternative: run full CLI/subprocess harness; adds infrastructure not required by behavior text. Rejection gate: no requirement for real OS/network/model output.
behaviors.test_description: Setup: invoke the real in-process harness assembly path with production-like fixtures. Assertion: assembled prompt text fails a leakage scan for phase-instruction patterns (i.e., none present). Failure caught: leakage introduced during interpolation/fixture merge despite clean base template.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: Behavior targets source-of-truth wiring between file loader, skill resolver, and invocation builder. Candidate tests: Integration: load real plugin/skill files and assert invocation context changes when files change (without prompt rule edits); catches hard-coded fallback bugs. Agentic alternative: require live model behavior validation; higher level only if correctness depends on model judgment. Rejection gate: text emphasizes "loads skill context from plugin/skill files" (wiring), not model-quality scoring.
behaviors.test_description: Setup: run invocation construction with real plugin/skill file inputs and a prompt lacking hard-coded phase rules. Assertion: resolved context includes file-derived skill content and differs when file content differs. Failure caught: harness silently using baked-in prompt instructions instead of file-backed skill context.

behaviors.description: The harness can run the same user-style prompt against an intentionally degraded or missing SKILL.md variant without changing test prompt text.
behaviors.justification: This is a harness configurability/state-isolation check across modules and filesystem fixtures; no model-quality dependence required. Candidate tests: Integration: parameterize skill-path fixture (good vs degraded/missing) with identical prompt text, assert runs execute both variants; catches prompt-coupling bugs. System alternative: execute via external CLI process; unnecessary unless subprocess-only behavior exists. Rejection gate: behavior is about run setup, not live model correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Setup: execute two in-process runs with identical user prompt and different skill-file fixtures (valid vs degraded/missing). Assertion: harness accepts both runs without prompt edits and records distinct skill variant under test. Failure caught: hidden coupling where prompt text must be changed to simulate degradation.

behaviors.description: When the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails under the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: Pass/fail depends on real model behavior changing with skill-content quality; deterministic stubs can hide this. Candidate tests: Agentic: same prompt, real model, compare baseline vs degraded skill context and assert degraded run violates structural expectation; catches true tautology regression. Integration alternative: stub model outputs fixed response; cannot detect whether skill degradation actually affects model decisions. Rejection gate: behavior explicitly says "behavioral test fails" when skill content is degraded under same prompt, which requires real model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Setup: run live-model test twice with identical prompt and harness, swapping only skill context (baseline vs degraded section removed). Assertion: degraded condition fails behavioral criteria while baseline passes. Failure caught: tests that still pass because behavior comes from hard-coded prompt text instead of skill content.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: Ordering bug is at boundary between model client, logger/artifact writer, and parser modules. Candidate tests: Integration: force parser failure on malformed output, assert raw artifact exists and matches pre-parse bytes; catches lost diagnostics. Unit alternative: test parser wrapper function with mock callbacks; may miss real pipeline order/wiring. Rejection gate: no need for model-quality evaluation; real network/subprocess not required.
behaviors.test_description: Setup: execute response-processing pipeline with a malformed model response fixture that triggers parse error. Assertion: diagnostic artifact containing raw output is written before parse/extraction step throws. Failure caught: parser runs first and drops/overwrites raw evidence.

behaviors.description: Assertions enforce structural behavior constraints (for example schema paths and role-specific field placement), not only keyword presence checks.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure boundary is assertion logic itself (schema/path validation rules). Candidate tests: Unit: feed crafted outputs where keywords exist but fields are misplaced, assert structural validator fails; catches false positives from keyword checks. Integration alternative: run full harness assertions; useful but not minimal for this behavior statement. Rejection gate: behavior describes assertion semantics, not infrastructure or live-model dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Setup: provide fixture outputs containing required keywords but incorrect schema paths/role placement to assertion function. Assertion: structural checks fail with targeted path errors while keyword-only check would pass. Failure caught: regression to superficial keyword matching.

behaviors.description: Under live model execution, structural assertions catch regressions that a keyword-only check would miss.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires live model execution; correctness depends on real output shape/content variations. Candidate tests: Agentic: run real model on regression-prone prompt, assert structural validator fails while keyword check passes; catches real-model near-miss outputs. Integration alternative: replay canned outputs; cannot prove detection under live nondeterministic responses. Rejection gate: phrase "Under live model execution" disqualifies pure Integration; Workflow not required because this is a single-step model response check.
behaviors.test_description: Setup: run live-model behavioral smoke test with user-style prompt and collect response. Assertion: structural validator flags schema/role-placement violation in cases where keyword presence remains true. Failure caught: real regressions masked by keyword-only assertions.
